Issues.Title: WinForms layout issue with CheckBox and ComboBox when using HighDpiMode.PerMonitorV2
Issues.Body:
### .NET version

.NET 10

### Did it work in .NET Framework?

Not tested/verified

### Did it work in any of the earlier releases of .NET Core or .NET 5+?

_No response_

### Issue description

When a UserControl contains a CheckBox and a ComboBox, enabling
`Application.SetHighDpiMode(HighDpiMode.PerMonitorV2);`
causes a height mismatch between the controls. The ComboBox appears slightly taller, which results in two visible lines at the bottom of the control.

This only occurs when PerMonitorV2 DPI awareness is enabled.

### Steps to reproduce
(A video and a VS project are attached below as demo)
Create a UserControl containing: one CheckBox and one ComboBox

Example startup code:
```
[STAThread]
static void Main()
{
    Application.EnableVisualStyles();
    Application.SetCompatibleTextRenderingDefault(false);
    Application.SetHighDpiMode(HighDpiMode.PerMonitorV2);

    Application.Run(new Form1());
}
```
Place both controls on a UserControl, aligned vertically.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e98643b-8c81-462f-844a-80f9233079b1
[CAD_VS2026.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25876676/CAD_VS2026.zip)

Issues.Title: Fix issue 14049：The title of the collection editor for custom controls in .NET Core is incorrect.
Issues.Body: Fixes #14049 ## Proposed changes - Add a helper (GetCollectionItemTypeNameForCaption) that detects a generic ControlProxy-like wrapper and unwraps the inner T when T derives from System.Windows.Forms.Control. - Add a test case to verify the created collection editor form caption contains the underlying control type name and does not contain ControlProxy\1`. ## Customer Impact - The title of the collection editor for custom controls in .NET Core show correctly. ## Regression? - Yes ## Risk - Min ## Screenshots ### Before ### After ## Test methodology - Manually ## Test environment(s) - 11.0.0-preview.2.26122.107 ###### Microsoft Reviewers: [Open in CodeFlow](https://microsoft.github.io/open-pr/?codeflow=https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/pull/14375)

Issues.Body:
**Issue:** Intermittent `BindingContext_UpdateBinding_UpdateListBindingWithoutDataMember_Success` test failure on x86 platform

**Fix:** Obtain a strong reference to `manager1` before calling `UpdateBinding`.

Issues.Title: Fix dark mode: CheckBox/RadioButton checked state invisible with Standard FlatStyle, ProgressBar BackColor leaks through
Issues.Body:
Two dark mode regressions fixed: CheckBox/RadioButton with `FlatStyle.Standard + Appearance.Button` renders no visible checked/unchecked distinction in dark mode; ProgressBar unexpectedly reflects user-set `BackColor`/`ForeColor` in dark mode (whereas Visual Styles suppress these in classic mode).

## CheckBox / RadioButton (#14347)

`OwnerDraw` was explicitly `false` for `DarkMode + Standard + Appearance.Button` to avoid `VisualStyleRenderer` HighDPI issues, handing rendering back to the native control — which doesn't differentiate checked state in dark mode.

**Fix:** `ButtonDarkModeAdapter` doesn't use `VisualStyleRenderer` at all, so the workaround is unnecessary. `CreateButtonAdapter()` now returns `ButtonDarkModeAdapter` in dark mode, and the `OwnerDraw` override is simplified to `base.OwnerDraw`.

- `CheckBox.cs` / `RadioButton.cs`: Remove dark-mode special-case from `OwnerDraw`
- `CheckBoxStandardAdapter.cs` / `RadioButtonStandardAdapter.cs`: `CreateButtonAdapter()` returns `ButtonDarkModeAdapter` when `Application.IsDarkModeEnabled`; removed now-redundant `private new ButtonStandardAdapter ButtonAdapter` cast

`ToPushButtonState` already maps `CheckState.Checked → PushButtonState.Pressed`, so the pressed visual correctly reflects the checked state.

## ProgressBar (#11938)

`SetWindowTheme(HWND, " ", " ")` disables Visual Styles in dark mode, making `PBM_SETBKCOLOR`/`PBM_SETBARCOLOR` effective — so user-set colors like `BackColor = Color.Red` bleed through. In classic mode Visual Styles silently suppress these.

**Fix:** Pin dark mode colors to `SystemColors.ControlDarkDark` (background) and `SystemColors.Highlight` (bar) regardless of the `BackColor`/`ForeColor` properties, matching classic-mode semantics where those properties have no visible effect.

- Added `s_darkModeBackColor = SystemColors.ControlDarkDark`
- `OnCreateControl`: after `SetWindowTheme`, explicitly send `PBM_SETBKCOLOR(s_darkModeBackColor)` and `PBM_SETBARCOLOR(s_defaultForeColor)`
- `OnBackColorChanged` / `OnForeColorChanged`: skip `PBM_SET*` when `IsDarkModeEnabled`
- `OnHandleCreated` / `UserPreferenceChangedHandler`: use conditional color selection (`IsDarkModeEnabled ? s_darkModeBackColor : BackColor`)

## Tests

Added `CheckBoxStandardAdapterTests` and `RadioButtonStandardAdapterTests` verifying that `CreateButtonAdapter` returns the correct adapter type per mode and that all paint methods are non-throwing in dark mode.

Issues.Title: Prioritize top open WinForms feature asks from issue backlog
Issues.Body:
This PR addresses the request to identify the most important open feature asks in `dotnet/winforms` and provide a prioritized implementation list.  
It delivers a demand- and readiness-weighted shortlist from the current open issue backlog.

- **Prioritization output**
  - Produced a ranked list of top asks, led by:
    1. Generic Host integration (`UseWindowsFormsLifetime`)
    2. WinForms Application Builder modernization
    3. App-level theming (light/dark/custom)
    4. Taskbar progress API support
    5. Control drawing lock/unlock API
    6. Vectorized drawing/bitmap operations
    7. Expanded `ListViewGroup` capabilities
    8. `DataGridView.Sort` overload for glyph direction

- **Ranking criteria applied**
  - Demand signals: issue discussion volume and community reactions
  - Strategic fit: modernization/performance/accessibility/platform parity
  - Execution readiness: milestone targeting, ownership signals, and triage labels

- **Deliverable characteristics**
  - Focused on open asks (primarily `api-suggestion` / `enhancement`)
  - Optimized for roadmap intake and planning review
  - No product/runtime source changes in this PR; this is backlog-prioritization output
